key_insight: A fully discrete least-squares splitting method for the 2D Monge-Ampère equation is proven to converge when paired with finite element Hessian reconstruction and local determinant projection.
engineering_relevance: The result provides a validated FEM framework for solving nonlinear PDEs with provable accuracy, supporting reliable simulation workflows in computational mechanics and geometry.
method: Reconstruct the Hessian with a finite element operator.
Project locally onto the determinant constraint from the Monge-Ampère equation.
Prove convergence using a discrete Miranda-Talenti estimate and standard approximation properties.
evidence_signal: The paper establishes local convergence of the iterative scheme and optimal-order convergence in an H^2-type norm, then confirms the rates with numerical experiments.
watch_item: The quadratic C0-interior penalty and DG variants require a smallness condition on |u|_{H^3(Ω)}, and the theory is limited to smooth 2D Dirichlet problems.
paper_title: Fully discrete least-squares splitting scheme for the Monge-Ampère equation: finite element analysis and convergence
